metadata.title: Selenium Automated Testing Suite
metadata.subtitle: Complete Build Documentation — From Scratch to Final Run

step.title: Install and Configure Cursor IDE
step.summary: Cursor is a VS Code fork with deeply integrated AI. All VS Code extensions work identically. Download and install before creating the project.
step.block: 1. Go to https://cursor.com 2. Click "Download for Windows" 3. Run CursorSetup.exe 4. Launch Cursor and sign up for free account
# Run once if PowerShell blocks script activation Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser

ext.name: Pylance
ext.purpose: Fast type checking and Selenium autocomplete

ext.name: Python Test Explorer
ext.purpose: Visual pytest tree with inline run buttons

ext.name: GitLens
ext.purpose: Git history, blame, and PR reviews

step.title: Verify Python and Create Project
step.block: cd %USERPROFILE%\Desktop mkdir selenium_suite cd selenium_suite # Then in Cursor: File -> Open Folder -> select selenium_suite

# Create venv python -m venv venv
# Activate — PowerShell venv\Scripts\activate
# Activate — CMD (fallback) venv\Scripts\activate.bat
# You should see (venv) in your prompt ✅
pip install selenium pytest pytest-html webdriver-manager allure-pytest pytest-xdist
selenium>=4.0.0 pytest>=7.0.0 pytest-html>=3.1.0 pytest-xdist>=2.5.0 webdriver-manager>=3.8.0 allure-pytest>=2.12.0

python -c "import selenium; print('Selenium version:', selenium.__version__)" python -c "import pytest; print('pytest version:', pytest.__version__)"
# Expected output: # Selenium version: 4.41.0 # pytest version: 9.0.2
mkdir config pages tests utils reports reports\screenshots type nul > config\__init__.py type nul > pages\__init__.py type nul > tests\__init__.py type nul > utils\__init__.py

node.desc: Product listing, sorting, add to cart

file.purpose: Centralises all settings. Uses environment variables for security.
file.block:
import os
class Config: BASE_URL = os.environ.get("APP_URL", "https://www.saucedemo.com") BROWSER = "chrome" IMPLICIT_WAIT = 10 EXPLICIT_WAIT = 20 HEADLESS = os.environ.get("HEADLESS", "false") == "true"
# Saucedemo public test credentials VALID_USER = "standard_user" LOCKED_USER = "locked_out_user" PROBLEM_USER = "problem_user" PASSWORD = "secret_sauce"
file.verify: python -c "from config.config import Config; print('BASE_URL:', Config.BASE_URL)" # Expected: BASE_URL: https://www.saucedemo.com

file.purpose: Foundation class all page objects inherit from. Provides explicit wait methods.
Login page locators and actions for saucedemo.com.
Product listing, sorting, add to cart, cart badge management.
Cart page item management — view, remove, continue, checkout.
3-step checkout. Uses element-based waits for reliable slow-page navigation.
file.lesson: Always wait for a VISIBLE ELEMENT on the destination page rather than waiting for URL changes. EC.url_contains() races against slow page loads. EC.presence_of_element_located() waits for proof the page has loaded.

file.purpose: Burger menu, logout, reset app state, top navigation bar.
Fixtures, hooks, auto-screenshot, startup banner, terminal summary.

key_features.feature: driver() fixture — fresh Chrome per test, auto-quit after
logged_in_driver() fixture — pre-logged-in driver for non-login tests
Auto-screenshot on every test failure via pytest hook
Chrome password manager disabled to prevent popup alerts
pytest_configure — prints suite banner at start
pytest_terminal_summary — prints rich results + saves JSON

file.block: [pytest] testpaths = tests addopts = -v --html=reports/report.html --self-contained-html --tb=short --metadata "Project" "Selenium Suite — saucedemo.com" --metadata "Environment" "QA" --metadata "Browser" "Chrome" markers = smoke: Quick sanity checks — run on every commit regression: Full regression suite — run nightly login: Login-related tests inventory: Product and cart tests checkout: Checkout flow tests navigation: Navigation and logout tests

command.title: Serve Allure Dashboard (needs Java + Allure CLI)

allure_setup.title: Install Allure on Windows
allure_setup.block:
# Step 1 — Install Scoop package manager Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser irm get.scoop.sh | iex
# Step 2 — Install Allure via Scoop scoop install allure
# Step 3 — Set JAVA_HOME (required by Allure) $env:JAVA_HOME = "C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-25.0.2.10-hotspot" $env:Path = $env:Path + ";" + $env:JAVA_HOME + "\bin"
# Make JAVA_HOME permanent [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", "C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-25.0.2.10-hotspot", "User")
# Verify java --version allure --version

issue.title: TimeoutException on Checkout Pages
issue.symptom: Tests in TestCheckoutOverview all throwing TimeoutException after clicking Continue or Finish.
issue.root_cause: EC.url_contains() races against slow page loads — the URL may not have updated yet when the assertion runs.
issue.fix: Wait for a VISIBLE ELEMENT on the destination page instead of the URL. EC.presence_of_element_located(FINISH_BTN) proves the Overview page loaded.
issue.block:
# ❌ WRONG — races against page load self.wait.until(EC.url_contains("checkout-step-two"))
# ✅ CORRECT — wait for actual element on destination page WebDriverWait(self.driver, 30).until( EC.presence_of_element_located(self.FINISH_BTN) )

issue.title: Chrome Password Manager Alert
issue.symptom: Chrome shows a data breach popup warning for "secret_sauce" — a known public test password.
issue.root_cause: Chrome's built-in password manager flags publicly known passwords as compromised.
issue.fix: Disable the password manager and leak detection via Chrome experimental options.

issue.title: PowerShell Execution Policy Blocks venv Activation
issue.symptom: venv\Scripts\activate throws "running scripts is disabled on this system".
issue.root_cause: Windows default execution policy prevents running unsigned PowerShell scripts.
issue.fix: Set execution policy for current user only, or use .bat fallback.
issue.block:
# Fix — set for current user only (safe) Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser
# Fallback — use .bat version (always works) venv\Scripts\activate.bat

issue.title: Test Logic Mismatch — saucedemo URL Access After Logout
issue.symptom: test_cannot_access_inventory_after_logout FAILED — saucedemo allows direct URL access after logout.
issue.root_cause: Saucedemo is a demo site — it intentionally does not implement session-based URL protection.
issue.fix: Remove the incorrect test. Replace with tests that verify what saucedemo actually does (login button visible, session cleared).
issue.block:
# ❌ WRONG — incorrect assumption about demo site assert "inventory" not in driver.current_url # saucedemo doesn't redirect
# ✅ CORRECT — test actual app behavior nav.logout() assert login.is_visible(login.LOGIN_BUTTON) # confirms session ended

issue.title: JAVA_HOME Not Set — Allure CLI Fails
issue.symptom: allure serve throws "JAVA_HOME is not set and no java command could be found".
issue.root_cause: Java was installed (Eclipse Adoptium JDK 25) but JAVA_HOME environment variable was not set.
issue.fix: Set JAVA_HOME to the JDK path both for current session and permanently.
issue.block:
# Current session $env:JAVA_HOME = "C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-25.0.2.10-hotspot" $env:Path = $env:Path + ";" + $env:JAVA_HOME + "\bin"
# Permanent [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", "C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-25.0.2.10-hotspot", "User")

marker.name: smoke
marker.description: Quick sanity — run on every commit
